title: Museum curator talk
explanation:
Museum curator talk

Listen to a curator introducing an exhibition.

Transcript

Curator: This exhibition does not present a single national story. Instead, it traces how trade, migration and conflict shaped the region over five centuries. Many objects on display have only recently been reinterpreted using new archival research. We invite visitors to question familiar narratives and notice what earlier exhibitions chose to omit.

questions.text: The exhibition avoids a single [*national story|shopping guide|sports timeline].
questions.feedback: Does not present a single national story.

questions.text: Some objects were reinterpreted using new [*archival research|social media|recipes].
Visitors are invited to notice what earlier exhibitions [*omitted|invented|sold].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The display covers five ____.  | *centuries | minutes | meters
The curator wants visitors to question familiar ____.  | *narratives | noodles | numbers
The approach is deliberately ____.  | *critical | careless | comic
Many items have been recently ____.  | *reinterpreted | recycled | removed
The talk is [*academic and accessible|purely technical|unrelated to history].
